papers.title: ReGuide: From Test-Time Guidance to Self-Improving Diffusion Policies
papers.abstract: Behavior-cloned diffusion policies are expressive but remain vulnerable to covariate shift: small deviations from demonstrated states can compound into task failure. Existing methods address this either by expanding the training distribution through expert corrections or synthetic augmentation, or by steering a frozen policy at test time with guidance from a learned model. The former can be expensive or assumption-dependent, while the latter discards the corrected trajectories after execution. We introduce ReGuide, a self-improving framework that treats guided rollouts as reusable on-policy recovery data. ReGuide first uses Phase-Conditioned Guidance (PCG) to generate corrective rollouts: it constructs phase-specific latent targets, applies guidance only in the drifted-but-recoverable regime, and guides through the estimated clean action to match the dynamics model's training distribution. Successful guided rollouts are then absorbed back into the policy through ReGuide-FT, which fine-tunes the current checkpoint, or ReGuide-FS, which retrains from scratch on the augmented dataset; the two can also be composed and iterated. On Robomimic Can, Square, Transport, and Tool Hang, ReGuide improves base-policy success by $1.3$--$7.7\times$, outperforms LPB in the test-time-only setting, and matched-data ablations show that the gains come from guided recovery data rather than additional rollouts alone.

papers.title: ViPSim: Collaborating Visual and Parameter Spaces for Consistent Long-Horizon Embodied World Models
papers.abstract: Embodied World Models (EWMs) have emerged as a scalable and risk-free paradigm for advancing embodied intelligence, enabling the safety-critical evaluation of Vision-Language-Action systems. However, their reliability as evaluation benchmarks and foundational simulators is often hindered by the representation gap between low-dimensional actions and high-dimensional video synthesis. This gap results in a lack of geometric correspondence, manifesting as accumulated trajectory drift and inconsistent robot-object interactions during long-horizon rollouts. To bridge this gap, we propose ViPSim, a framework that achieves consistent long-horizon generation through the synergistic collaboration of Visual and Parameter Spaces. We define the Visual Space as a domain of explicit spatial priors, integrating pixel-aligned projections of end-effector pose, camera perspectives, depth-informed scene geometry, and robotic morphological masks to provide dense structural grounding. Concurrently, the Parameter Space serves as a domain of numerical drivers, injecting raw action sequences and camera matrices to provide precise motion guidance. By unifying these two spaces, ViPSim ensures that the generated states are simultaneously anchored by geometric boundaries and steered by numerical commands. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ViPSim is backbone-agnostic and significantly enhances trajectory consistency. Notably, our approach exhibits emergent capabilities in generating complex interactions with deformable objects (e.g., cloth folding) and maintains robust performance in out-of-distribution and cross-embodiment scenarios, providing a high-fidelity foundation for the automated evaluation and predictive control of embodied agents.

papers.title: A Physics-Grounded Benchmark for Multi-Agent Dynamics in World Models
papers.abstract: Generative world models hold immense promise as scalable simulators for autonomous systems, particularly for synthesizing rare but safety-critical multi-agent interactions, such as vehicle collisions. However, current evaluation paradigms index heavily on visual fidelity and semantic alignment, leaving a critical blind spot: they cannot reliably quantify whether generated dynamics actually obey the fundamental physical laws required for reliable simulation. Assessing this physical plausibility is inherently difficult due to a lack of physical metrics and the challenge of extracting metric-scale kinematics from uncalibrated video rollouts. To bridge this gap, we introduce CrashTwin, a physics-grounded evaluation framework designed to stress-test the physical trustworthiness of world models. CrashTwin couples a diverse dataset of multi-agent collision scenarios, comprising 25K controllable synthetic and 12K in-the-wild real-world collision sequences with a novel calibration-free reconstruction pipeline, enabling the recovery of 3D physical attributes directly from world model rollouts. We propose a diagnostic suite that systematically evaluates three dimensions: spatio-temporal consistency, momentum and kinetic energy conservation, and world-dynamics integrity. Extensive benchmarking of state-of-the-art models reveals a crucial insight: high perceptual quality frequently masks severe physical violations during complex interactions. By quantitatively exposing these failure modes, CrashTwin provides a vital diagnostic tool for developing physically grounded world models capable of reliable real-world simulation.

papers.title: RIPA: Sensory-Vector Prompt Injection Attacks on LLM-Controlled ROS 2 Robots
papers.abstract: We present RIPA, the first systematic multi-channel empirical study of prompt injection attacks delivered through the sensory pipeline of a ROS 2-based LLM-controlled robotic system. Across 100 independent runs per injection variant on five LLMs spanning four model families and parameter scales from approximately 4B to approximately 284B (DeepSeek-V4-Flash, Llama-3-8B-Instruct-Lite, Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo, Qwen 2.5-7B-Instruct-Turbo, Gemma-3n-E4B), we identify model-specific vulnerability profiles that do not follow a monotonic scaling trend: Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo exhibits 100% attack success rate (ASR) across all injection variants, while Llama-3-8B-Instruct-Lite and Qwen 2.5-7B-Instruct-Turbo resist direct-override injection (0% ASR), and the smallest model evaluated (Gemma-3n-E4B, approximately 4B) matches the 70B model's vulnerability profile, indicating that robustness is model-specific rather than scale-dependent. We propose a hybrid semantic firewall that achieves 0% ASR against known injection patterns with no false positives on a preliminary benign set (0/20 commands) but exhibits a 10.2% trial-weighted bypass rate (58/570 trials; N equals 30 per payload across 19 obfuscation payloads) against adversarially obfuscated attacks, exposing a critical gap between rule-based and semantic defense layers. We further introduce three sensory injection channels: visual (Channel 1, via OCR), audio (Channel 2, via Whisper STT), and LiDAR sensor context poisoning (Channel 3). We show that Channel 3, which injects fabricated obstacle data into the robot environment-state representation at the LLM system-prompt level, achieves 100% ASR across all variants on DeepSeek-V4-Flash. We also contribute a firewall bypass taxonomy spanning 19 obfuscation payloads across five categories. All code, data, and results are publicly available.

papers.title: Reachability Guarantees for Cart-Pole Swing-Up and Stabilization
papers.abstract: The cart-pole swing-up is a canonical benchmark for nonlinear control of underactuated systems, yet an end-to-end guarantee linking the global swing-up maneuver to the local stabilizer is seldom formalized. We present a reachability analysis of a switched energy-based/LQR controller that certifies convergence to the upright equilibrium from a compact set of initial conditions. The swing-up law is derived from an energy-error Lyapunov function; canceling the autonomous conservative term yields a strictly sign-definite Lyapunov derivative, and convergence follows from LaSalle's invariance principle. We also propose an augmented Lyapunov function to regulate the steady-state cart velocity to zero, for which we establish almost-global convergence. For the controller handoff, a switching region is designed to lie strictly within the LQR region of attraction, formally certifying the swing-up-to-stabilization transition. Numerical simulations corroborate the theoretical analysis.

executive_summary: New planning and control frameworks push long-horizon, cluttered, and contact-rich manipulation to larger scales (LAMP, Chronos, WARP, ConCent, HJ-SafeDMP).
Whole-body humanoid and hybrid mobile-manipulation capabilities advance rapidly via synthetic data, closed-loop behavior models, and domain adaptation (VLK, ReactiveBFM, AnyBody, KYON, FADA).

Vision-language(-action) models mature into practical manipulation and navigation systems, with improved tokenization, robustness, memory, and test-time self-improvement (ZR-0, SA-VLA, Event-VLA, T^2VLA, ReGuide).

Navigation research spans learning-based planners, world-action models, and swarm/UAV methods that emphasize memory, risk-awareness, and timing robustness (CORE Planner, SWAM, SPACE, RoamFlow, LNN-Fly, multi-UAV/quadrotor methods).
Touch, visuo-tactile fusion, and structured perception/memory emerge as key enablers for contact-rich and long-horizon tasks (HTT, TAP-VLA, TacGen, YOTO, analytic concept-centric memory, HUMEMBR).
Institution-highlighted work includes CMU’s scalable multi-robot manipulation planner (LAMP) and UC Berkeley’s thermal LiDAR-inertial SLAM (PL-LIT) extending operation to adverse visual conditions.

institution_matches.title: LAMP: Long-Horizon Adaptive Manipulation Planning for Multi-Robot Collaboration in Cluttered Space
institution_matches.overview: Proposes LAMP, a long-horizon multi-robot manipulation framework that couples a learned generative manipulation model with two planners: LAMPA* for systematic search in joint object–robot space and LAMP-Lazy for real-time replanning via deferred evaluation. It scales to dense, cluttered scenes where prior RL or surrogate-planning approaches fail, enabling coordinated contact-rich manipulation over extended horizons.

institution_matches.title: PL-LIT: A LiDAR-Inertial-Thermal SLAM Using Point-Line Features and Thermographic Mapping
institution_matches.overview: Introduces PL-LIT, a tightly coupled LiDAR–inertial–(thermal or RGB) SLAM system with online photometric calibration and deep point–line feature extraction suited to low-contrast thermal imagery. Using an ESIKF formulation and a probabilistic thermal-intensity voxel map, it delivers robust long-range odometry and supports real-time thermal anomaly detection in adverse visual conditions.

institution_matches.title: Understanding LLM Intervention Explanations in Multi-Party Human-Robot Interaction
institution_matches.overview: Analyzes 610 explanations generated by an LLM-based orchestrator controlling two social robots in three-human group discussions to understand when and why robots intervene. Thematic analysis shows explanations emphasize facilitation (agreement, participation, flow), with role-specific patterns between "mover" and "opposer" robots, informing explainable, multi-party HRI design.

topic_matches.title: LAMP: Long-Horizon Adaptive Manipulation Planning for Multi-Robot Collaboration in Cluttered Space
topic_matches.overview: LAMP tackles long-horizon multi-robot manipulation in highly cluttered environments by combining a learned generative manipulation model with two planners: LAMPA* for exhaustive search in joint object–robot space and LAMP-Lazy for efficient real-time replanning. It jointly reasons about contact formations, coupled dynamics, and collision avoidance, solving dense scenarios that defeat prior RL and surrogate-planning baselines.

topic_matches.title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
topic_matches.overview: VLK creates large-scale vision–language–kinematics supervision by reconstructing indoor environments with 3D Gaussian splats, synthesizing navigation and interaction trajectories, and rendering egocentric views post hoc. A learned policy outputs short-horizon whole-body trajectories for a Unitree G1 humanoid, enabling sim-to-real loco-manipulation from purely synthetic interactions.

topic_matches.title: ReactiveBFM: Reactive Closed-Loop Motion Planning Towards Universal Humanoid Whole-Body Control
topic_matches.overview: ReactiveBFM integrates a generative behavior foundation model with closed-loop planning, using scheduled prefix sampling to train error-recovery behaviors and asynchronous replanning to match high-frequency tracking. On the Unitree G1, it achieves robust, text-conditioned whole-body motions including zero-shot moving-target reaching, significantly outperforming open-loop cascaded baselines under perturbations.

topic_matches.title: KYON: Semi-Modular Wheel-Legged Quadruped With Agile Bimanual Capability
topic_matches.overview: Presents KYON, a wheel–leg hybrid quadruped with a bimanual upper body designed for loco-manipulation in complex terrain. A whole-body control framework plus RL-based policies drive dynamic locomotion and independent bimanual manipulation, validating agile, reconfigurable mobile manipulation in unstructured environments.

topic_matches.title: WARP: Whole-Body Retargeting for Learning from Offline Human Demonstrations
topic_matches.overview: WARP introduces a whole-body-aware retargeting pipeline that maps human pose data to consistent robot actions for mobile manipulators by explicitly modeling embodiment differences. Using a closed-form SEW (Shoulder–Elbow–Wrist) solver and lazy base control, it yields precise trajectories that support zero-shot whole-body mobile manipulation from offline human videos without teleoperation.

topic_matches.title: OpenSPM: An Environment-Transferable Robotic Key Spatial Pose Memory and Closed-Loop High-Frequency Flow-Matching Action Generation Model
topic_matches.overview: OpenSPM stores object-centric key spatial poses as a persistent memory, retrieved via language and transferred to new scenes with SE(3) transforms to guide manipulation. A lightweight conditional flow-matching model generates high-frequency closed-loop action chunks with residual corrections, achieving >1 kHz effective control and strong performance on LIBERO-GOAL tabletop tasks.

topic_matches.title: CORE Planner: Contextual-memory Oriented Reinforcement-learning in Unknown Environments for Robot Navigation
topic_matches.overview: CORE Planner combines a sparse visibility-graph representation with a Transformer-based contextual memory to support RL-based navigation in large, unknown environments. It reduces path length and improves sim-to-real transfer over traditional and learning baselines, achieving zero-shot real-world navigation without fine-tuning.

topic_matches.title: AnyBody: Free-Form Whole-Body Humanoid Control from Arbitrary Keypoint Guidance
topic_matches.overview: AnyBody learns a latent motion space for humanoids that can be driven by any subset of body keypoints specified at deployment, enabling flexible tracking and free-form control. A privileged teacher tracker is distilled into a latent motor prior, and a keypoint transformer aligns arbitrary keypoint sets to this latent, supporting teleoperation and downstream loco-manipulation behaviors via a lightweight residual corrector.

topic_matches.title: HJ-SafeDMP: Hamilton-Jacobi Reachability-Guided Dynamic Movement Primitives for Provably Safe Robot Motion
topic_matches.overview: HJ-SafeDMP augments Dynamic Movement Primitives with a learned Hamilton–Jacobi Control Barrier Value Function that acts as a closed-form safety filter on top of nominal motions. Trained offline via finite-difference HJ recursion with conformal prediction calibration, it yields provably safe, robust arm motions with orders-of-magnitude faster execution than optimization-based CBF-QPs while preserving DMP adaptability.

topic_matches.title: HUMEMBR: Learning Human Routines for Predictive Embodied Navigation
topic_matches.overview: HUMEMBR builds structured, continuous memory of human activities from long-term robot observations to support routine-aware navigation and question answering. By separating ongoing memory construction from query-time retrieval, it improves long-horizon reasoning about where and when people are likely to be present and is validated both in simulation and on physical robots in real environments.
